006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.util.regex.*;
022
023/**
024 * Shared test-support utilities for validating XML output.
025 */
026public final class XmlTestUtils {
027
028   private XmlTestUtils() {}
029
030   /**
031    * Validates that the whitespace is correct in the specified XML.
032    *
033    * @param out The XML to validate.
034    * @throws Exception If the whitespace/indentation in the XML is incorrect.
035    */
036   @SuppressWarnings({
037      "java:S112"  // Generic exception throw required; checked exception wrapping would obscure test intent.
038   })
039   public static void checkXmlWhitespace(String out) throws Exception {
040      if (out.indexOf('\u0000') != -1) {
041         for (var s : out.split("\u0000"))
042            checkXmlWhitespace(s);
043         return;
044      }
045
046      var indent = -1;
047      // Whitespace-validation patterns.  The lookbehind on the trailing '>' distinguishes start tags ('(?<!/)')
048      // from self-closing tags ('(?<=/)') in a single linear pass, avoiding the super-linear backtracking of the
049      // previous '.*'/nested-quantifier forms (java:S8786).
050      var startTag = Pattern.compile("^(\\s*)<[^/>][^>]*(?<!/)>$");
051      var endTag = Pattern.compile("^(\\s*)</[^>]+>$");
052      var combinedTag = Pattern.compile("^(\\s*)<[^/>][^>]*(?<=/)>$");
053      var contentOnly = Pattern.compile("^(\\s*)[^\\s\\<]+$");
054      var lines = out.split("\n");
055      try {
056         for (var i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
057            var line = lines[i];
058            var m = startTag.matcher(line);
059            if (m.matches()) {
060               indent++;
061               if (m.group(1).length() != indent)
062                  throw new Exception("Wrong indentation detected on start tag line ''" + (i+1) + "''");
063               continue;
064            }
065            m = endTag.matcher(line);
066            if (m.matches()) {
067               if (m.group(1).length() != indent)
068                  throw new Exception("Wrong indentation detected on end tag line ''" + (i+1) + "''");
069               indent--;
070               continue;
071            }
072            m = combinedTag.matcher(line);
073            if (m.matches()) {
074               indent++;
075               if (m.group(1).length() != indent)
076                  throw new Exception("Wrong indentation detected on combined tag line ''" + (i+1) + "''");
077               indent--;
078               continue;
079            }
080            m = contentOnly.matcher(line);
081            if (m.matches()) {
082               indent++;
083               if (m.group(1).length() != indent)
084                  throw new Exception("Wrong indentation detected on content-only line ''" + (i+1) + "''");
085               indent--;
086               continue;
087            }
088            var twc = tagWithContentIndent(line);
089            if (twc != -1) {
090               indent++;
091               if (twc != indent)
092                  throw new Exception("Wrong indentation detected on tag-with-content line ''" + (i+1) + "''");
093               indent--;
094               continue;
095            }
096            throw new Exception("Unmatched whitespace line at line number ''" + (i+1) + "''");
097         }
098         if (indent != -1)
099            throw new Exception("Possible unmatched tag.  indent=''" + indent + "''");
100      } catch (Exception e) {
101         printLines(lines);
102         throw e;
103      }
104   }
105
106   /**
107    * Returns the leading-whitespace indent width of a line that is a single tag-with-content line.
108    *
109    * <p>
110    * A tag-with-content line is one that — after any optional leading whitespace — starts with an opening tag and
111    * ends with a closing tag, such as <c>&lt;foo&gt;bar&lt;/foo&gt;</c>.  This is a linear-time replacement for the
112    * <c>^(\s*)&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;.*&lt;/[^&gt;]+&gt;$</c> regular expression that avoids its super-linear backtracking.
113    *
114    * @param line The line to test.
115    * @return
116    *    The number of leading whitespace characters on the line, or <c>-1</c> if the line is not a single
117    *    tag-with-content line.
118    */
119   private static int tagWithContentIndent(String line) {
120      var n = line.length();
121      var w = 0;
122      while (w < n) {
123         var c = line.charAt(w);
124         if (c != ' ' && c != '\t' && c != '\n' && c != '\u000B' && c != '\f' && c != '\r')
125            break;
126         w++;
127      }
128      if (n - w < 2 || line.charAt(w) != '<' || line.charAt(n - 1) != '>')
129         return -1;
130      var open = line.indexOf('>', w);          // End of the opening tag.
131      if (open - w < 2)
132         return -1;
133      var close = line.lastIndexOf("</");        // Start of the final closing tag.
134      if (close <= open || close + 3 > n - 1)
135         return -1;
136      for (var j = close + 2; j < n - 1; j++)    // Closing tag name must not contain '>'.
137         if (line.charAt(j) == '>')
138            return -1;
139      return w;
140   }
141}
